expectations.text: In turn 1, agent proposes a multi-column, grid, or dashboard-style layout for displaying multiple metrics — not a single stacked list
expectations.evidence: Turn 1: 'a card grid layout: each metric gets its own card with the value prominently displayed... Something like 3-4 cards in a row that collapse to 2 columns on mobile.'

expectations.text: In turn 1, does NOT create any extension files — waits for user answer before building
expectations.evidence: Turn 1 only proposes design and asks 3 questions. Files are created in Turn 2 after user specifies metrics.

expectations.text: Final layout uses CSS grid, multi-column flexbox (flex-wrap), or explicit column structure to display metrics side-by-side rather than a single vertical stack
expectations.evidence: content.html uses display: grid with grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr), responsive breakpoints at 900px (2-col) and 480px (1-col).

expectations.text: Final widget shows 4 distinct metrics (active users, NPS score, open tickets, avg response time)
expectations.evidence: All 4 metric cards present in HTML: Active Users, NPS Score, Open Tickets, Avg Response Time. Each with distinct icon, color coding, and trend indicator.

expectations.text: Each metric has intentional visual treatment — large prominent number with smaller label, color coding, icon, or trend indicator
expectations.evidence: Each card has: colored SVG icon in tinted background, uppercase label (0.8125rem), large value (clamp 1.5-2rem, 700 weight), color-coded trend badge with arrow. 4 distinct color themes (indigo, green, amber, blue).

expectations.text: Layout is designed for quick scanning — metric values are visually dominant (larger font-size or bolder weight) over labels and secondary info
expectations.evidence: Values: clamp(1.5rem, 3vw, 2rem) font-weight: 700. Labels: 0.8125rem font-weight: 500 in muted gray. Trend badges: 0.75rem. Clear visual hierarchy.

expectations.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment
expectations.evidence: Both files exist in outputs/widgets/metrics-dashboard/. HTML is a fragment with <style>, <div>, and <script> — no <html>/<head>/<body> wrappers.
